nodes.headline: With proven ROAS, scale spend infinitely; optimize funnel first, then increase budget.
nodes.text:
# Google Ads Optimization

> With proven ROAS, scale spend infinitely; optimize funnel first, then increase budget.

## Key Numbers
- Current ROAS: 13:1 at $5k/month spend
- After funnel optimization: estimated 26:1 ROAS
- Potential spend scale: $5k → $30k/month with same ROAS
- Currently upping Google ads spend from $3k to $5k/month

## Framework
- If channel returns $5 per $1, budget should scale to 'as many dollars as possible'
- Reinvest all returns back into the same channel (compounding growth)
- Fix landing page and ads before scaling spend (80/20 principle)

## Common Mistakes
- Business owners spend $1k/month on proven 10:1 channels due to psychological barriers ('I feel poorer spending $1k/day')
- Not verifying correct attribution before scaling; data must be clean
- Ignoring branded search terms, allowing competitors to bid on your name

## Claims
- Google Ads currently yielding 13:1 ROAS with $5k/month spend; optimizing landing page and funnel could double ROAS to 26:1, enabling scale from $5k to $30k/month spend. — [building_a_2_500_000_business_for_a_stranger_in_36_minutes_OQf2Ba-Lp_4.txt]
- If an ad channel returns $5 for every $1 spent, budget should scale to 'as many dollars as you possibly could' and reinvest returns back into that channel. — [building_a_2_500_000_business_for_a_stranger_in_36_minutes_OQf2Ba-Lp_4.txt]

## Connections
- Could scale from 2-3 leads weekly to daily with proper spending; optimize funnel first with proven ROAS, then increase budget infinitely. _(connects to: Ad Spend Scalability)_
- Google Ads and GMB optimization both improve visibility and ROI through proven funnels and local reach. _(connects to: Google My Business Optimization)_

---
_Source: building_a_2_500_000_business_for_a_stranger_in_36_minutes_OQf2Ba-Lp_4.txt · 2
Improve close rate first via in-person selling before raising prices; refining process order of operations unlocks hidden margin.

nodes.headline: Buy with seller financing or subject-to, sell to buyer in LLC, hold 30-40 years of monthly cash flow at 60% cash-on-cash returns.
nodes.text:
# Alternative Mortgage Models

> Buy with seller financing or subject-to, sell to buyer in LLC, hold 30-40 years of monthly cash flow at 60% cash-on-cash returns.

## How-To / Method
- Buyer obtains 2% seller financing or sub-2 mortgage
- Seller transfers property to buyer's LLC
- Owner collects $25K upfront + $800/month × 30-40 years

## Claims
- Easy Dwell model: buy property with seller financing or sub-2, transfer to buyer via LLC, collect $25K upfront + $800/month cash flow for 30-40 years — [i_m_so_confused_walk_me_through_how_you_make_money_fTV3PbWAfWQ.txt]

---
_Source: i_m_so_confused_walk_me_through_how_you_make_money_fTV3PbWAfWQ.txt · 1 claim(s)_

nodes.headline: Third Marshmallow Fallacy: delaying gratification forever wastes resources; learn when to reap, not just sow; risk-adjust future outcomes.
nodes.text:
# Gratification Timing

> Third Marshmallow Fallacy: delaying gratification forever wastes resources; learn when to reap, not just sow; risk-adjust future outcomes.

## Framework
- Early career: longer delay = bigger outcomes (dose relationship)
- Later career: must learn when to accept gratification
- Winners get reinforced for delaying more and more; harder to stop delaying

## Key Takeaway
- Not all delayed outcomes are worth delaying; risk-adjust
- $100M today > uncertain $big-amount 10 years away (5 years to use resources)
- It's okay to make money; it's okay to just make money

## Examples
- Personal training client: $4K/month in cash; not scalable, not high-multiple; enabled survival during gym launch
- Content creator with 1M followers: built audience, never asked for anything; must learn when to monetize

## Implications
- Invest vs. consume must be in the middle somewhere, not extremes
- Forever sowers die with unused resources; must draw personal line of when to reap

## Claims
- Third Marshmallow Fallacy: delaying gratification forever means you die with unused resources; must learn when to reap, not just when to sow — [26_harsh_lessons_i_learned_in_2025_YONIUpk-kAs.txt]
- Early career: longer delay = bigger outcomes (dose relationship); later career: risk-adjust outcomes; $100M today > $uncertain 10 years away — [26_harsh_lessons_i_learned_in_2025_YONIUpk-kAs.txt]
- Personal training client paying $4K/month in cash allowed survival during gym launch; scalable purity sacrificed for ability to eat and delay gratification on business — [26_harsh_lessons_i_learned_in_2025_YONIUpk-kAs.txt]
- Content creators building audiences for years without asking for anything; invest vs. consume must be in the middle somewhere — [26_harsh_lessons_i_learned_in_2025_YONIUpk-k

nodes.headline: Communities reinforce undefined language through social approval.
nodes.text:
# Self-Reinforcing Patterns

> Communities reinforce undefined language through social approval.

## Framework
- Join community that values certain words
- Say those words → get approval, acceptance, belonging
- Keep saying words even though you've never defined them
- Believe the words because the social reward is real

## Claims
- People say words they've never defined but believe them because of the positive social response they get; if a community rewards certain phrases (manifestation, frequencies), people adopt those words without understanding them. — [why_i_hate_the_word_mindset_5xWG6jb6vB8.txt]

---
_Source: why_i_hate_the_word_mindset_5xWG6jb6vB8.txt · 1 claim(s)_

nodes.headline: Fear dissolves when crystallized into specific, detailed worst-case scenarios; most imagined catastrophes are survivable and unlikely.
nodes.text:
# Fear and Risk-Taking

> Fear dissolves when crystallized into specific, detailed worst-case scenarios; most imagined catastrophes are survivable and unlikely.

## Framework
- Write down worst-case scenario in excruciating detail, step-by-step
- Realize it's not as bad when crisp vs. when hazy
- Others care far less about your failures than you think (everyone is self-focused)

## Key Takeaway
- Dare greatly because nothing you own is permanent; you're renting everything
- Commitment must be to effort, not outcome; control what you can, roll the dice on the rest
- No great achievement comes from fear of others' opinions

## Claims
- Fear exists only when hazy, not when crisp—write down worst-case scenarios in excruciating detail to realize outcomes are not that bad — [26_harsh_lessons_i_learned_in_2025_YONIUpk-kAs.txt]
- Set public, ambitious goals (e.g., $100M launch goal) and dare greatly; people respect visible commitment to goals, not outcomes — [26_harsh_lessons_i_learned_in_2025_YONIUpk-kAs.txt]
- Marcus Aurelius quote: you fear losing what never belonged to you; all assets are borrowed, temporary, and should not induce fear — [26_harsh_lessons_i_learned_in_2025_YONIUpk-kAs.txt]

## Connections
- Fear dissolves when crystallized into worst-case scenarios; most are survivable. _(connects to: Fear As Illusion)_
- Courage is short time between perceiving risk and taking action; fear dissolves when worst-case is detailed and quantified. _(connects to: Courage Risk Interval)_
- Crystallizing worst-case scenarios reveals most catastrophes are survivable; replacing fear with math removes emotional paralysis. _(connects to: Risk Assessment)_

---
_Source: 26_harsh_lessons_i_learned_in_2025_YONIUpk-kAs.txt · 3 claim(s)_
